title: Kyoto Geography - Location, Terrain & Climate
content:
Kyoto is a city located on the island of Honshu in Japan, known for its rich cultural heritage and stunning natural beauty.

[FACTBOX]
Land Area: 827 km², about the size of the city of Toronto
Highest Point: Mount Hiei, 848 meters
Lowest Point: Kamo River, 10 meters
Coastline: landlocked
Borders: Osaka, Nara, Shiga, Fukui, and Mie Prefectures
Climate Type: temperate
[/FACTBOX]

[SECTION] Location & Borders [/SECTION]
Kyoto is situated in the Kansai region of Japan, approximately 50 kilometers northeast of Osaka. The city is nestled in the Yamashiro Basin, surrounded by mountains on three sides, including the Higashiyama and Nishiyama ranges. To the north lies the Tanba Highlands, while the Uji River flows through the city and empties into the Katsura River, which in turn flows into the Yodo River. Kyoto is landlocked, but its proximity to the Sea of Japan and the Pacific Ocean has a moderating effect on its climate.

Kyoto is bordered by several prefectures, including Osaka to the south, Nara to the east, Shiga to the north, Fukui to the northeast, and Mie to the southeast. The city's strategic location has made it an important cultural and economic center for centuries, with easy access to the rest of Japan and the Asian continent. The surrounding mountains and rivers have also played a significant role in shaping the city's history and development, with many temples, shrines, and gardens built along the waterways and in the foothills of the mountains.

[SECTION] Terrain & Landscape [/SECTION]
Kyoto's terrain is characterized by its location in a basin surrounded by mountains. The city's landscape is dominated by the Higashiyama and Nishiyama ranges, which rise to elevations of over 800 meters. The mountains are covered in dense forests, with many temples and shrines built along the mountain slopes. The city itself is situated on a flat plain, with many rivers and streams flowing through it, including the Kamo, Katsura, and Uji rivers. The surrounding countryside is also home to many **rice paddies** and **tea plantations**, which are an important part of the local economy and culture.

The terrain in Kyoto is also marked by many **valleys** and **gorges**, which have been carved out by the rivers and streams over millions of years. The most famous of these is the **Arashiyama** valley, which is known for its stunning natural beauty and historic landmarks, including the **Tenryu-ji** temple and the **Bamboo Grove**. The city's terrain has also been shaped by human activity, with many **canals** and **dams** built to control the flow of water and provide irrigation for the surrounding countryside.

[SECTION] Terrain & Landscape [/SECTION] (continued)
In addition to its natural beauty, Kyoto's terrain has also played a significant role in its cultural and economic development. The city's location in a basin surrounded by mountains has made it an important center for trade and commerce, with many merchants and travelers passing through the city on their way to and from other parts of Japan. The surrounding countryside is also home to many **quarries** and **mines**, which have provided stone and other materials for the city's many temples, shrines, and gardens.

[SECTION] Climate By Season [/SECTION]
[TABLE]
| Season | Months | Temperature Range | Rainfall | Conditions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Spring | March to May | 10-20°C | 100-150 mm | Mild and sunny, with cherry blossoms in bloom |
| Summer | June to August | 25-35°C | 150-200 mm | Hot and humid, with frequent rain showers |
| Autumn | September to November | 10-20°C | 100-150 mm | Cool and comfortable, with vibrant fall foliage |
| Winter | December to February | 0-10°C | 50-100 mm | Cold and dry, with occasional snowfall |
[/TABLE]

[SECTION] Best Time To Visit [/SECTION]
[CALLOUT] Best months: March to May and September to November. Why: These periods offer mild and comfortable weather, with fewer crowds and lower prices. Avoid: June to August, as the summer heat and humidity can be oppressive, and December to February, as the winter cold and lack of daylight can make sightseeing less enjoyable. [/CALLOUT]

[SECTION] Natural Highlights [/SECTION]
- **Arashiyama Bamboo Grove**: a stunning natural wonder with towering bamboo stalks and a serene atmosphere
- **Kinkaku-ji Temple**: a famous temple with beautiful gardens and a peaceful pond
- **Fushimi Inari Shrine**: a historic shrine famous for its thousands of vermilion torii gates
- **Philosopher's Path**: a scenic walking path lined with cherry blossom trees and temples
- **Kurama Onsen**: a natural hot spring resort town located in the mountains north of Kyoto
- **Uji River**: a scenic river that flows through the city and offers stunning views and boat rides
